country_risk_title: Refugee hosting pressures amid recurrent droughts and limited resources
connection_summary: Zero refugee enrollment in TVET is plausibly linked to broader system and service-delivery strain associated with hosting a large refugee population under tight resource constraints. When national services (including education/training) are stretched, refugee-targeted outreach, intake, documentation support, language/bridging assistance, transport, and stipend/fee mechanisms needed to convert eligibility into actual enrollment can be under-prioritized or poorly funded, resulting in non-implementation of the refugee beneficiary stream.

country_risk_title: Political constraints on civil society may limit broader governance and stabilization engagement
connection_summary: Achieving refugee enrollment often depends on implementation partnerships with non-state actors (CSOs/NGOs, refugee community organizations) for identification, outreach, case management, and trust-building. Country-level constraints on civil society space could reduce the ability or willingness of such actors to operate at scale or to coordinate openly, weakening last-mile engagement with refugees and plausibly contributing to persistent zero enrollment despite a PDO focus on refugees.

country_risk_title: Refugee hosting pressures amid recurrent droughts and limited resources

country_risk_title: Political constraints on civil society may limit broader governance and stabilization engagement
